def: s.m. = ghī, q.v.

ety: S. घृत+पूर+कः; or Prk. घिअउल्लओ = S. घृत+उल+कः
def: s.m. A small kind of cucumber (much used as a condiment).

ety: ghī, q.v.+wālī, fem. of wālā, or S. आलः, with euphon. w
def: adj. Containing much ghī; rich (milk).
adj. Like ghī; — soft; tender; — s.m. A pompion, Cucurbita lagenaria
subs: or ghīyā-torī, s.f. The Luffa pentandra
s.m. An instrument for cutting and cleaning pumpkins (syn. kaddū-kash)

def: adj. Containing or yielding much ghī (milk); rich (milk).
adj. & s.m. (used as last member of compounds), Striking; killing, destroying; removing; — killer, murderer, destroyer, &c.
s.m. (dialec.) = گهوڙا ghoṛā, q.v.
s.m. 1˚ = ghoṅghā; — 2˚ = ghoghī, qq.v.
s.f. = ghoṅghī or ghūṅgī, and ghūngaṭ, qq.v.
v.t. = ghoshnā, q.v.
v.t. = ghoshnā, q.v.
s.m. (Dakh.) = gokhrū, q.v.
s.m. Buttermilk (syn. ćhāćh).
s.f. (dialec.) Purslane, Portulaca oleracea; — a kind of reddish-white sea-fish ( = gholā).

ety: ghola, rt. of gholnā, q.v.+S. कः; — or perf. part. of gholnā
def: s.m. An intoxicating potion made of opium or bhaṅg; — a mixture; solution; — a kind of reddish-white sea-fish
subs: (-ko), To throw into confusion or disorder; to involve in difficulty; to harass, worry, injure.

ety: perf. part. of gholnā, and = Prk. घोलिअओ; S. घोलित+कः, a Prk. form of घूर्णितः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Mixed; stirred, stirred up; disturbed; — thick; turbid
v.t. = ghulānā, q.v.

ety: rts. of gholnā and ghumāʼonā = ghumānā, qq.v.
def: s.m. Evasion; subterfuge; procrastination.
s.m. A mixing; mixture.

ety: ghol˚ = Prk. घोल(इ) = S. घोलय (ति), rt. घोल्, a Prk. form of घूर्न्
def: v.t. To mix (with a liquid); to dissolve, melt; to stir together, stir up; to shake about, to agitate briskly.

ety: ghol(nā) + wā = āwā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. A solution; mixture; gruel; slops; — an intoxicating drink made of opium ( = gholā, q.v.)
subs: To make a watery mess (of a thing), make too thin; — to practise evasion; to procrastinate, to delay; — to reduce to a semi-fiuid state; to do injury (to), to injure (a person).

def: s.m. A cockle; cockle-shell, Cochlea helix; a snail; snail-shell; any spiral univalve shell (syn. āʼīṅṭhā).
s.m. = ghoṅgā, q.v.
s.f. dim. of ghoṅghā = ghoṅgā, q.v.

s.f. The tying the end of a blanket in a knot and so placing it on the head as a protection against rain; a hooded cloak or covering; — a cloth folded up and put on the head (as a defence against the rain); — a coarse woollen (or other) cloth doubled over breadthwise and sewn at the bottom and sides (the top being left open); a sack, bag, pouch, wallet; a pocket (usually ghoghī); — the palmer-worm; a caterpillar; a kind of grub destructive to grain, &c. (usually ghūṅgī, or ghūṅgrī, or ghuṅgrī).
s.f. dim. of ghoṅgā, q.v.

ety: ghus, rt. of ghusnā, q.v.+S. आलयः; cf. the Mārwāri ghusyālo
def: s.m. Nest (of a bird); — (met.) a small house, a crib, cabin.
= H گهونٿا घोंटा ghoṅṭā = ghoṭ, and ghoṭā, qq.v.
= H گهونٿ घोंट ghoṅṭ = ghoṭ, and ghoṭā, qq.v.
v.t. = ghoṭnā, q.v.
s.m. A cloth (or a blanket), with one end folded like a sugar-loaf to cover the head, worn over the body in cold or rainy weather (syn. ghoṅghī).
s.m. (in comp.) corr. of ghoṛ = ghoṛā.
s.m. = ghuṛ (like which it is used as first member of compounds).
s.m. (Dakh.) A hole
subs: s.m. A species of lizard, a skink, or guana, Lacerta scincus.

def: s.m. A horse; — (in chess) the knight; — a clothes-horse; — hammer (of a gun)
subs: To put a horse to full speed
or ghoṛe-par zīn bāṅdhnā or kasnā, To saddle a horse
(bandūq-kā), To raise the hammer (of a gun), to cock (a gun)
(bandūq-kā), To pull the trigger (of a gun)
(-ko), To put (a mare) to a stallion
(-par), To put a horse to full speed; to gallop a horse (at or after); to charge
To force a horse to its utmost speed
s.m. A cast horse; a screw.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Awful, terrific, frightful, terrible, horrible, dreadful; violent, vehement; — deep (as colour, or as sleep); — s.m. The terrible, an epithet of Śiva; — awfulness, horribleness; — a horrible action; — horror; trepidation; dread, awe; — sound or roll (of a drum); — roar; thunder; — dirt
subs: (˚ra+āk˚), adj. & s.m. or ghorākr̤iti (˚ra+āk˚), adj. & s.f. = ghor-rūp, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Of horrible or frightful appearance; terrifying, terrific
adj. (f. -ā), Of terrible aspect or form; frightful, hideous, grim; — s.m. A hideous appearance
v.n. To conceal (one's) dislike or indignation
s.m. An awful (or a terrifying) sound
s.m. Tartarus; hell with all its horrors.

def: s.m.(?) A post, prop (for supporting a roof, &c.
s.f. = S گهورتو घोरत्व ghoratva, s.m. Frightfulness, terribleness, horribleness, horror.
s.m. = S گهورتا घोरता ghoratā, s.f. Frightfulness, terribleness, horribleness, horror.

ety: Prk. घोडअओ (with w inserted) = S. घोटक+कः
def: s.m. (rustic) = ghoṛā, q.v.
adj. = aghorī q.v.
s.f. A mare; — a clothes-horse; a saddle-rack; — a machine for making vermicelli; — wooden pincers (used in circumcision)
(prob. for gholnā), s.f.(m.?) A kind of refreshing beverage.
s.m. Name of a caste of herdsmen or milkmen; — an individual of that caste, a herdsman, a milkman (see ghosī)
subs: a common name for a Kāyasth

def: s.m. Indistinct noise, tumult; sound (of a drum, &c.); roar (of an animal, &c.); — language; — a station of herdsmen; — (f. -ā), a caste of cowherds, &c. (see ghos).
v.t. To proclaim; to preach.
v.t. To proclaim; to preach.
v.t. To speak or utter aloud; to proclaim; — to repeat, recite (prayers, or a lesson, &c.); to iterate.
s.m. A cowherd, herdsman; a milkman (esp. a Moḥammadan milkman, one of those who are converts from the caste of Ahīrs).
s.f. The wife of a Ghosī; a (Moḥammadan) milk-woman.
s.m. = ghoṅslā, q.v.
s.f. Polish; — a sense of suffocation.

s.m. An intoxicating infusion of hemp-leaves (dry leaves of the hemp-plant ground in water, with spices, &c.); — barley husk (given to donkeys, &c.); — a wooden pestle, muller, or bruiser; — a polishing stone (an instrument of jewellers, &c. to rub socket-gold, or to polish gems or ornaments).
s.m. (f. -ī), A horse (see ghoṛā).

ety: ghoṭ˚ = Prk. घुट्ठ = S. घृष्ट, p.p.p. of rt. घृष्; — or caus. of ghuṭnā
def: v.t. To rub, rub down; to pound, bruise, grind, to pulverize (medicine, &c. in a mortar); to levigate; — to press, squeeze (the throat), to throttle, to choke ( = galā ghoṭnā); — to rub and stir (in order to mix); to mix; to solve, to dissolve (by mixing, or boiling or cooking); to boil down (milk, &c.); — to smooth, to polish (by rubbing with a hard and smooth body); to glaze (paper, &c.); to calender; — to shave; — to strive after, exert (oneself) for, to be busy with; to plod at; — to sift, to investigate; — to repeat; to read over and over again
subs: (-kā), To press or squeeze the throat (of), to throttle.

def: s.m. A muller, a rubber (of wood or stone); — a polishing-stone ( = ghoṭā, q.v.).

ety: S. घुण्ट+nā = lā = Prk. डओ or लओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. The ankle (see ghūṭī).
s.f. A rubber, muller, &c. ( = ghoṭnā, q.v.).
adj. Polished, smooth (words).
s.f. The nose.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Smelled; smelling; — s.m. Smelling; smell; — odour; — the nose
subs: s.m. (lit. 'pleasant to the nose'), Fragrance, odour, perfume
(˚ṇa+in˚), s.m. The organ of smell, the nose; the sense of smell.

def: part. Smelt, smelled at, sniffed.
s.f. Aversion, dislike, abhorrence; contempt, disdain; — lassitude (see ghin).
s.m. corr. of grīshma, q.v.
adj. Anointed with clarified butter, smeared with ghī
subs: vulg. ghr̤it-kuwār, s.f. The plant Aloe indica (which is supposed to resemble a virgin in delicacy and elegance; — cf. ghīkuwār).

def: s.m. (dialec.) A bastion; an outwork (before the gate of a walled town or village).
s.m. Hooting, hoot (of an owl); cooing (of a dove); — an owl; a turtle-dove.
s.f. 1˚ = ghūghī, q.v.; — 2˚ = ghoghī, or ghuṅghī or ghoṅghī, qq.v.
s.m. = ghugghū, q.v.
s.f. A turtle-dove.
s.m. = ghugghū or ghughu, q.v.

ety: ghughu, q.v.+S. कः; or S. घूक+कः (with w inserted)
def: s.m. An owl; — a turtle-dove; — (met.) a blockhead.
s.m. = ghughuʼā, q.v.
adj. = ghuṅghar-wālā.
s.f. A kind of herb that grows during the rains on high ground.
s.f. = ghūṅyāṅ, or ghūyāṅ, q.v.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Melted, dissolved; mixed; — ripe, mellow (fruit, &c.); — soft; flexible
subs: part. adj. Closely united (with); close, thick, intimate

def: s.f. Melting, dissolving; fusion; softness, mellowness; mildness; tenderness (e.g. ghulāwaṭ-kī āṅkh, 'an eye (or glance) of tenderness').
s.f. Melting, dissolving; fusion; liquefaction.
v.t. To melt, dissolve; to mix (in or with, -meṅ); to cause to waste away; — (dialec.) to coax, wheedle, allure, entice, seduce, deceive.
adj. Melting, dissolving; causing to waste away; — (dialec.) alluring, seductive, deceitful, &c.
s.f. Melting, dissolving; fusion; softness, mellowness; mildness; tenderness (e.g. ghulāwaṭ-kī āṅkh, 'an eye (or glance) of tenderness').
adj. Dissolved; mixed; melted (in kindness); — in close union, intimate ( = ghulā-milā, q.v.s.v. ghulā)
subs: To be on very intimate terms; to live in close union or in harmony.

def: v.n. To be or become dissolved; to dissolve, melt; to be mixed (with, -meṅ), be blended or united (with); — to become soft, or mellow, or ripe (as fruit, &c.); to rot; — to become lean, to waste away (from grief or sickness); — to wrestle
v.t. To cause to be melted or dissolved; — to cause to melt or dissolve; to cause to mix (with a liquid).
s.m. Running round a pole (a game amongst children); — adv. Round, about; back.

ety: caus. of ghūmnā; — ghumā˚ = ghumāw˚ = Prk. घुम्मावे˚ = S. घूर्ण+आपि, caus. augment
def: v.t. To cause to turn or revolve; to turn round, whirl round; to wheel; to roll; to brandish, flourish; — to give (an animal, &c.) a turn round; to take round; to circulate; to encircle; — to lead astray; to beguile, delude
subs: v.t. To lead (one) a pretty round; — to prevaricate

ety: prob. akin to ghūmnā; cf. ghumaṛnā
def: s.f. Gathering (of clouds).
v.n. To gather (as clouds).
s.m. prop. ghumāʼo, q.v.

ety: ghumā(nā)+āʼo or āʼū = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Turning round; whirling round, &c. (see ghumānā); — a turn round; — as much land as can be ploughed by one pair of bullocks in a day; a measure of land varying from one fifth to three-fourths of an acre.
v.n. (dialec.) = ghūmnā, q.v.
s.f. = ghumrī, q.v.
s.f. = ghumrī, q.v.

ety: by redupl. of ghūm, rt. of ghūmnā+S. कः
def: s.m. Going round, revolving, revolution; — subterfuge, evasion, prevarication; — doubt, suspense, hesitation.

ety: by redupl. of ghūm, rt. of ghūmnā + S. आल+कः and इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Revolving, circling; round; — having great girth or width; full, wide, loose (as a skirt or robe).
v.n. To go round, to revolve; — to practise subterfuge; to prevaricate; to beat round the bush.
v.t. To lead (one) round about; to make (one) dance attendance; to practise evasion or procrastination with, to put (one) off from time to time.

ety: by redupl. of ghūm, rt. of ghūmnā + S. आल+कः and इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Revolving, circling; round; — having great girth or width; full, wide, loose (as a skirt or robe).
s.f. = ghumrī, q.v.
v.n. (colloq. & Dakh.) = ghūmnā, q.v.

ety: Prk. घुम्मणअं; S. घूर्णनीयं, rt. घूर्ण्
def: v.n. To turn, turn round, revolve, whirl, spin (as a top, &c.), wheel, circle; to go round, to go round and round, to swim (as the head, e.g. sir ghūmnā); to be or become giddy or dizzy; — s.m. Turning round, whirling, &c.
s.f. = ghumrī, q.v.

ety: ghum˚ = ghūm˚, rt. of ghūmnā+rā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. The plant Phlomis indica, or P. zeylanica (?) — a species of insect (cf. bhramar).
adj. Turning round; having turns or twists (the word is only met with in the following)
subs: s.m. A ḥuqqa (syn. pećwān); — a tobacco-pipe.

def: v.n. (dialec.) = ghumaṛnā = ghūmnā; — v.t. = ghumānā, q.v.
s.f. Vertigo, giddiness, swimming of the head.
s.f. = ghumrī, q.v.

ety: imperf. part. of ghūmnā; — and = Prk. घुम्मंतओ, rt. घुम्म˚ = S. घूर्ण
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Turning round, revolving
subs: A revolving line; radius (of a circle).

def: v.t. To cause to be turned round, or to be revolved, &c.; to have (a thing) taken round, &c. (see ghumānā, and ghūmnā).
s.m. Rancour, malice, spite.
s.m. Rancour, &c. ( = ghun, q.v.).

s.m. A kind of insect (destructive to wood, grain, and flour); wood-louse; weevil; — the dust or powder of worm-eaten wood or grain; — the pangolin or armadillo; — (met.) grief, sorrow; canker; — any wasting disease
subs: (-meṅ), To be attacked by the wood-louse or the weevil; to be worm-eaten; to be the prey of a wasting disease.

def: adj. Malicious, &c. ( = ghunā, q.v.).
part. adj. (f. -ī), Destroyed by the wood-louse, or by the weevil (wood, grain, &c.), worm-eaten; — internally decayed; hollow.
adj. (f. -ī), Malicious, spiteful, revengeful, rancorous; — sullen; — designedly or perversely silent, designing; — secret, cautious.
adj. corr. of ghanākshar = ghanāksharī, q.v.s.v. ghan.
s.f. A round button; a tag; — a kind of herb that grows in rice-fields after the crop is cut (camels are very fond of it; and it is used in medicine)
subs: (-kī), To button; — to put a button (on, meṅ)
'Button or knot of the heart;' doubt, perplexity.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = ghuṇḍī, q.v.
s.m. A curl; frizzy hair
subs: v.t. To curl; to frizz (the hair)
or ghūṅgaryālā, adj. (f. -ī), Curled, curly, frizzy (hair).

def: adj. (f. -ī) = ghūṅgar-wālā, q.v.s.v. ghūṅgar.
s.m. = ghūgas, q.v.
s.f. (Dakh.) = ghūṅgaṭ or ghūṅghaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = ghūṅghaṭ, q.v.
s.m. (rustic) = ghūṅgaṭ.
s.m. A rattling toy; a (child's) rattle.
s.f. The small red and black seed of Abrus precatorius (syn. ratī or rattī, q.v.).
s.f. = ghuṅgćī, q.v.
s.m. = ghūṅgar, q.v.
v.n. To be broken or defeated (an army)
subs: s.f. A veiled woman; a woman who has the edge of her wrapper or mantle drawn over her face.

def: adj. & s.f. Drawing a veil or a wrapper over the face; prudish; — a prude.
s.f. = ghuṅgćī, q.v.
s.f. = ghuṅgćī, q.v.
s.m. = ghuṅgrū, q.v.
s.m. = ghūṅgrū = ghuṅgrū, q.v.

s.f. The tying the end of a blanket in a knot and so placing it on the head as a protection against rain; a hooded cloak or covering; — a cloth folded up and put on the head (as a defence against the rain); — a coarse woollen (or other) cloth doubled over breadthwise and sewn at the bottom and sides (the top being left open); a sack, bag, pouch, wallet; a pocket (usually ghoghī); — the palmer-worm; a caterpillar; a kind of grub destructive to grain, &c. (usually ghūṅgī, or ghūṅgrī, or ghuṅgrī).
s.f. = ghūṅgnī, q.v.

ety: ghūṅg˚ = S. घूर्ण+क+nī, prob. = S. अन्न+इका
def: s.f. Wheat, gram, or pulse boiled whole and in the husk; a sort of stir-about of grain (esp. wheat, or gram).
s.f. = ghūṅgī, q.v.
s.f. = ghūṅgī, or ghoṅghī, qq.v.
s.f. = ghūṅgī, or ghoṅghī, qq.v.
s.m. A small bell; a string of small bells worn round the wrist or ankle; — morris-bells; — the death-rattle (syn. gharrā)
s.m. = ghuṅgrū, q.v.
adj. Malicious, &c. ( = ghunā, q.v.).
s.f. = ghūyāṅ, q.v.
s.f. = ghūyāṅ, q.v.
v.n. See گهننا ghunnā.
v.n. To be blighted by the weevil (grain, &c.); to be worm-eaten, &c. = ghun lagnā, q.v.s.v. ghun
s.f. = ghus, q.v.; — s.m. = ghūs or ghūṅs, q.v.
= H گهونسا घूंसा ghūṅsā = ghūs, and ghūsā, qq.v.
= H گهونس घूंस ghūṅs = ghūs, and ghūsā, qq.v.
s.m. = ghūṅsī, or ghūṅsiyā, qq.v.
adj. = khunsī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Given to taking bribes; corrupt; — one who takes bribes (syn. ghūs-lene-wālā).
adj. & s.m. Given to taking bribes; corrupt; — one who takes bribes (syn. ghūs-lene-wālā).
v.n. = ghusnā, q.v.
v.n. (dialec.) = ghusnā, q.v.

ety: Prk. घुट्टं or घोट्टं; S. ग्रस्तं, rt. ग्रस्
def: s.m. A gulp; a draught, a pull (at a pipe, &c.)
subs: To drink (or take) a draught; to gulp:-ghūṅṭ-ghūṅṭ pīnā, To drink by gulps
(-kā), To take a gulp or a draught; to take a pull (at a pipe, &c.)

def: s.m. corr. of gūṅṭh, q.v.
s.m. A kind of hill-pony.
v.t. = ghuṭānā, q.v.
s.m. corr. of ghūṅṭ, q.v.
s.f. = ghuṭṭī, q.v.
v.t. To gulp, drink, swallow, guzzle
adj. Hidden, dark, &c. (i.q. gup, q.v.)
v.t. = gubhānā, q.v.
s.m. Sweepings, &c. ( = ghūrā, q.v.); — (local) loose sandy soil
subs: s.m. Dues levied (in Bundelkhand) on every sharer and under-tenant in proportion to the whole expenses incurred during the year (it is so called as all kinds of miscellaneous items are included).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = ghūr, 'sweepings,' &c.

ety: i.q. ghoṛ, contrac. of ghoṛā, q.v.
def: s.m. A horse (used in comp.)
subs: s.f. A vehicle drawn by a horse; a four-wheeled carriage drawn by horses
s.m. Horse-riding; being mounted on horseback
s.m. (f. -ī), A mounted soldier; a trooper; a rider, horseman
s.f. Riding on horseback; — the bridegroom's riding on horse-back in a marriage procession; — cavalry; — a field-battery (of horse-artillery)
or ghuṛ-sāl, or ghuṛ-sālā, s.m. A stable
s.m. A cannon or gun carried on a horse.

def: s.m. Staring; stare; fixed gaze; — frowning; frown
subs: s.f. Mutual staring or gazing; — exchange of amorous glances.

def: s.m. Sweepings, rubbish; — soil, manure; — a dust-heap, dung-hill; — a temporary fire-place; a stove.
s.f. Bridge (of a musical instrument).
v.t. To frighten or terrify with cries; to growl at, snarl at; to snap at; to snub; to browbeat; to threaten, menace, frown at; to chide, rebuke, reprimand.
v.t. = H گهرکي घुरकी ghurkī, s.f. See ghuṛaknā, and ghuṛkī.

ety: prob. akin to S. घूर्ण; cf. H. bhulasnā, and jhulasnā
def: v.t. To curl (syn. peć denā); — v.n. To curl, to be curled.
s.m. = ghurat, q.v.
s.m. A cattle-pen.
s.m. = ghuṛ-bāhā, q.v.s.v. ghuṛ.
For this and other words beginning with ghuṛ, which are not given below, see s.v. گهڙ ghuṛ.
s.f. A twist (in thread, &c.), tangle; entanglement.

s.m. The mole-cricket, Gryllus gryllotalpa; — a phagedenic ulcer, Herpes exedens (so called from its burrowing in the flesh as the mole-cricket does in earth or in sand); — a species of woodpecker (found all over India), Picus maharattensis.

ety: ghurghurā˚ = S. घुर्घुर+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.n. To rumble; to grumble; to growl, to snarl; to snort; to snore.
v.n. = ghurghurānā, q.v.
s.f. A small, or an undersized, mare; — prop (of a verandah, &c.).
s.m. A dust-heap, dung-hill.
v.t. To frighten or terrify with cries; to growl at, snarl at; to snap at; to snub; to browbeat; to threaten, menace, frown at; to chide, rebuke, reprimand.
s.f. Growling, snarling; threat, menace; frown; browbeating; rebuff, rebuke
s.f. = H گهرکنا घुरकना ghuraknā, v.t. See ghuṛaknā, and ghuṛkī.
v.t. = ghūrnā, q.v.
v.n. To go round, to roll round; to roll the eyes about, to look round or about; to share; — v.t. To gaze intently on, to stare at; to ogle; — to look angrily upon, to frown at.
v.n. To snore; to snort; to grumble; to growl, snarl.
s.m.(f. ?), The tendo Achillis.
s.f. Rolling; revolving; whirling; — vertigo, swimming in the head, dizziness, giddiness.
v.n. To growl (as a dog, &c.); to snarl; to purr (as a cat, &c.).

ety: from ghus, rt. of ghusnā, q.v.; cf. ghūsā
def: s.m. The bandicoot rat, Mus malabaricus, or M. giganteus; — a bribe
subs: (-ko), To give a bribe (to), to bribe
or lenā, To take a bribe or bribes

def: s.f. Entering (forcibly), pushing (into), penetrating; forcible entrance

ety: for ghussā; — ghusa or ghus˚, rt. of ghusnā, q.v.+S. कः
def: s.m. A thump or blow of the fist, a buffet; — the fist; — a blow, shock (syn. ṣadma); — the bandicoot rat ( = ghūs)
subs: or ghūsā lagānā, or ghūsā pilānā (-ko), To give (one) a blow with the fist
or ghūsam-ghāsā, s.m. Fisticuffs; boxing; thumping and pulling
To fight with the fists; to box.

ety: caus. of ghusnā; — ghusā˚ = Prk. घुसावे˚ = S. घुष ( = घृष्) + आपि caus. aug.
def: v.t. To cause to enter or penetrate; to thrust or force (into, -meṅ); to stuff (in), to cram; to penetrate, &c. (see ghuseṛnā)
s.f. = khusaṛ-phusaṛ, q.v.

ety: by redupl. of ghusaṛ, rt. of ghusaṛnā, q.v.
def: s.f. (colloq.) Cramming, stuffing.
s.m. A young owl.
s.f. A woman of the Ghosī caste; the wife of a Ghosī, q.v.
v.t. To force (into, -meṅ), to thrust (into), to cram, stuff (into); to run or stick (into); to pierce; to insert; to insinuate; to foist

ety: prob. fr. S. घृष्ट; — or fr. the trans. ghuseṛnā, q.v.
def: v.n. To be forced or thrust (in, -meṅ), to be stuffed (in); — to thrust oneself (in), to enter forcibly ( = ghusnā).

ety: ghus˚, fr. ghusnā, q.v.+ak, fr. S. कृ+ī = S. इका
def: s.f. A bold forward woman; a wanton or unchaste woman.

ety: ghus˚ = Prk. घुस˚, fr. S. घुष् = घृष्
def: v.n. To be thrust (in, -meṅ), be forced (in); — to thrust or push oneself (in), to enter forcibly; to rush (in); to pierce or penetrate (in or into); — to enter uninvited, unprivileged, or unwarranted (into or amongst, -meṅ), to intrude; to interfere, to meddle (in)
subs: v.n. To come rushing (in, -meṅ); to thrust oneself (in)
(-meṅ), To thrust oneself (in); — to rush headlong or rashly (into a place or an affair)

def: v.n. (Dakh.) = ghusnā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of creeping grass with a yellow flower, bearing a bitter fruit (it is used as a condiment for horses, but is considered poisonous to man).
s.m. = ghūsā, 'blowʼ, q.v.
s.m. = ghūṅṭ, q.v.
s.f. Polishing; — polish; smoothness.
s.f. corr. of khuṭāʼī = khoṭāʼī, qq.v.
s.m. The knee ( = ghuṭnā, q.v.).
s.f. Stopping, or stoppage (of the breath); suffocation; — sultriness.
v.t. To rub clean; to polish, furbish; to shave clean, &c.
s.m. Short drawers, reaching to (and tight about) the knees.
s.f. = ghuṭṭī, or ghūṅṭī, q.v.
s.f. The ankle.
s.f. Land which has been under a rice-crop.

ety: ghuṭ˚ = Prk. घुट्ठ = S. धृष्ट, p.p.p. of rt. धृष्; see ghoṭnā
def: v.n. To be pounded; to be ground; to be rubbed, polished, furbished; to be squeezed or pressed (the throat); to be stopped (the breath); to be suffocated; — to be shaved clean; to undergo tonsure; — to be well boiled or cooked; — to be thoroughly mixed (in or with, -meṅ); to be close friends (with); to be engaged in conversation (with); — to be spread (over, -meṅ), to fill, pervade
subs: (-kā), 'The throat to be rubbed or squeezed,' to be throttled, to be choked or suffocated.

ety: S. घुट+nā = lā = Prk. डओ or लओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. The knee
subs: or ghuṭnoṅ-ke bal ćlnā, v.n. To crawl on the (hands and) knees (as a child)
To bend the knees, to kneel
To place the head on the knees (from shame, or grief, &c.).

def: v.t. = ghūṅṭnā, q.v.

ety: i.q. ghuṭnā, with इका for कः
def: s.f. The knee
subs: or ghuṭniyoṅ calnā = ghuṭnoṅ ćalnā, q.v.s.v. ghuṭnā.

def: s.f. A medicine consisting of aloes, spices, and borax (given to new-born infants to clear out the meconium)
subs: (-kī), 'To form an ingredient of (one's) ghuṭṭī'; to be a thing to which one is used from infancy.

def: s.m. A coarse kind of cotton (obtained from the ends of reeds, &c.); — the carpenter-beetle (an insect which penetrates wood; — cf. ghurghurā); — an insect found in water or marshy land (which is used as a bait).
s.f. The esculent root Arum colocasia (syn. kaććū).
s.f. The esculent root Arum colocasia (syn. kaććū).

ety: for A. غيبة, v.n. fr. ig̠tāba, 'he spoke evil of (him
def: s.f. Evil speech respecting a person behind his back (generally, with truth; and thus opposed to bǒhtān, q.v.), backbiting, detraction, slander, libel
subs: (-kī), To speak ill of one behind his back, to backbite, slander, traduce.

def: adj. & s.m. Detractory, slanderous, &c.; — a backbiter, secret complainer, detractor, slanderer.

ety: v.n. fr. غبّ 'to come on alternate days,' &c.
def: s.f. A tertian fever or ague.
adj. Miry, dirty, filthy.

ety: gic-pić = gać-pać, q.v.+Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. The Pleiades.
s.m. (dialec.) = gidh, q.v.
adj. (Dakkhinī) = giḍḍā, q.v.

ety: i.q. gīdh, or giddh; P. also gīd
def: s.m. A kite; a vulture.
s.m. (dialec. or rustic) = gīdaṛ, q.v.
s.m. A jackal, Canis aureus
s.f. A female jackal.
s.f. A female jackal.
adj. & s.m. = guṭṭā and giṭkā or guṭkā, qq.v.
s.m. A vulture.
adj. & s.m. = giḍḍā, q.v.
s.m. A vulture (i.q. giddh).

ety: gīdh˚ = Prk. गिध्द(इ) = S. गृध्य(ति), rt. गृध्
def: v.n. To be or become greedy (after); to be attracted (by); to be tamed, to become familiarized
adj. & s.m. Timid, cowardly; — stupid, silly, idiotic; — blockhead, dolt, idiot
subs: s.m. A stupid ass, a blockhead ( = gīdī).

def: s.m. contract. of geh, q.v.
s.f. A kind of small scolopendra that abounds in the rains (syn. kan-salāʼī).
adj. = gijgijā, q.v.

ety: by redupl. of gij = gić = gać; cf. gić-pić
def: adj. = gilgilā, q.v.
s.f. Earth, mud, clay, bole
subs: s.f. Armenian bole; — red earth, ruddle
s.f. An embankment; a causeway; — cost or repairs of an embankment, &c.
s.f. Lute, cement, mud cement (used to close the mouth of a vessel)

lit. 'To throw earth on earth,' to throw heaps of earth immediately on a corpse (whereas, the usual custom is, after depositing the corpse in the grave, to lay planks over it, and then fill up the remaining cavity with eartl
ex: Flowers have bloomed in the gardens.

def: s.f. Dampness, moisture ( = gīlā-pan)
s.m. = گله gila, q.v.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala). Ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *gʷel- (“throat”). Cognate with Latin gula (“throat”), English gullet, and Persian گلو (galu, “throat”).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Moist, damp, wet, humid; soaked with moisture
subs: s.m. A kind of edible bulb

def: s.m. A species of wild creeper, Mimosa scandens.
s.m. Complaint; lamentation; reproach, blame; accusation; remonstrance
subs: s.f. Bringing forward (or making) a complaint; — gila-guẕārī karnā, To make a complaint.

def: s.m. A covering, an envelope, a wrapper, a case, cover, sheath, scabbard; a pillow-case; a quilt; — a pellicle; enclosing membrane; calyx (of a flower); the prepuce
s.m. A kind of box or receptacle for pān leaves; or for flakes of betel-leaf.
s.m. = H گلہري गिलहरी gilahrī, s.f. A squirrel
subs: s.m.f 'Squirrel-killer'; the field-kite; the dwarf-eagle, Arpula pennata.

def: s.f. = A گلہرا गिलहरा gilahrā, s.m. A squirrel
subs: s.m.f 'Squirrel-killer'; the field-kite; the dwarf-eagle, Arpula pennata.

def: s.f. (dialec.) 1˚ = gilahrī; — 2˚ = gillī, qq.v.; — adj. = gilī, q.v.

ety: gīlā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. = gīlāpan, q.v.

ety: for A. غلالة, v.n. fr. غلّ 'to cause (a thing
def: s.m. A garment that is worn next the body beneath another garment (or beneath a coat of mail), an under waistcoat.
prop. n. Name of a country lying to the south of the Caspian sea and north of 'Irāq.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to (or coming from) Gīlān; — a native of Gīlān.

ety: gīlā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Moistness, dampness, wetness, humidity.
s.m. A glass, a tumbler; a mug.
s.m. = gilṭī, q.v.
s.m. = guṛać, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گلو (gulō). Cognate with Sanskrit गल (gala, “throat, neck”). Doublet of گلا (galā).

def: s.m. The flower (or the pod) of the mahuʼā or Bassia latifolia (cf. guleṅdā).

ety: prob. S. गिर, or गिल (rt. गॄ) + Prk. उल्लिआ = S. उल+इका
def: s.f. Betel-leaf prepared and folded up (ready for eating), a flake of betel-leaf (syn. bīṛā; khīlī).

ety: prob. S. गिर, or गिल (rt. गॄ) + Prk. उल्लिआ = S. उल+इका
def: s.f. Betel-leaf prepared and folded up (ready for eating), a flake of betel-leaf (syn. bīṛā; khīlī).
s.m. Prepared clay; clay-mortar; dirt, mud (syn. gārā).

ety: for A. غلاظة, inf. n. of غلظ 'to be thick, or gross,' &c.
def: s.f. Thickness, spissitude, grossness, bulkiness, coarseness; roughness, ruggedness; hardness; — roughness of manner, rudeness, churlishness; — (in Urdū) dirtiness, filthiness, filth, dirt, dung, ordure, offal.
s.f. (dialec.) = gilahrī, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. gīlā; or fr. galā = galita by redupl.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Wet, moist, clammy; slimy, viscous; soft, pulpy; squashy (as over-ripe fruit, &c.; syn. gijgijā).
adj. Of earth, made of earth or clay, earthy, earthen, clayey, argillaceous.
s.m. A garment made of goat's hair or wool; a blanket (syn. kammal); a carpet or rug (to lie down upon).

ety: inf. n. of غلّ 'to be affected with rancour,' &c.
def: s.m. Hatred, rancour, malevolence, malice, spite; insincerity, dishonesty, perfidy, fraud, trick; envy
subs: s.m. Fraud, deceit; — apprehension; objection, hesitation
adv. Without guile; without hesitation, frankly, boldly.

def: adj. (dialec.) = gīlā, q.v.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala). Ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *gʷel- (“throat”). Cognate with Latin gula (“throat”), English gullet, and Persian گلو (galu, “throat”).

def: s.m. corr. of گلاوه gilāwa, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of g̠ulām), Boys; boys who attend the virtuous in paradise.
s.f. Gilding; silver-gilt; electro-plate.
s.f. A protuberance; gland; a hard glandular swelling, tumour, schirrus.
s.f. = g̠ulel, q.v.

ety: for A. غلظة, inf. n. of غلظ 'to be or become thick,' &c.
def: s.f. Thickness, grossness, &c. ( = g̠ilāz̤at, q.v.); roughness, rudeness; enmity; filthiness.
s.f. Dimity; coarse cloth.

ety: contrac. of āgīn, rt. of āgandan; — prep. ā+Zend rt. kan = S. खन्
def: A suffix (denoting 'full of, or affected with, or possessed of') added to nouns to form possessive adjectives (e.g. surma-gīn, 'full of surma'; g̠am-gīn, 'sorrowful, sad').

ety: perf. part. of ginnā, &c.; — and = Prk. गणिअओ; S. गणित+कः, rt. गण्
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Numbered, counted, reckoned, &c. (see ginnā)
subs: (or napā) shorbā, prov. 'Counted (or a limited number of) lumps of meat (necessitate) a measured supply of soup' (applied to one who is exact in his expenses to a degree of penury; and to one whose income is hardly equal to his expenditure); — living from hand to mouth; — cut your coat according to your cloth.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

ety: inf. n. of غاني g̠ānā, 'to talk or say what is pleasing,' iii of غني; see g̠anā
def: s.f. Singing, vocal music; a song; an air, a tune.

ety: caus. of ginnā: — ginā˚, contrac. of gināw˚ = Prk. गणावे = S. गण+आपि, caus. aug.
def: v.t. To cause to count or number; to cause to reckon up; — to cause to be counted, &c. ( = ginwānā, q.v.).

ety: prob. S. गन्ध+आलः, or रः; cf. the syn. gandailā
def: s.m. A kind of insect which is very destructive to growing 'gram' and arhar.

ety: fr. S. गिन्दुक+auṛā = Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: s.m. A cake of solid sugar (usually distributed among the relatives at marriages).

ety: prob. S. गिन्दुक, or गेण्डु+र+इका; but cf. giṛurī
def: s.f. = guṛarī or giṛurī, q.v.
s.f. A rope of twisted grass or twine (made to support a round-bottomed jar; — syn. giṛurī; īṅḍūʼā; jūnā).
s.f. A kind of dish, generally used in Moharram (syn. mālīda, q.v.).
s.f. A species of Julus (syn. kan-salāʼī).

ety: gīṇj˚ = S. घृष्; or fr. S. ग्रभ्; Prk. गेज्झ˚
def: v.t. To rub, to mash with the hand; to crumple.
v.t. dialec. = ghićolnā, and gīṅjnā, qq.v.

ety: gin˚ or gaṇ˚ = Prk. गण(इ) = S. गणय(ति), rt. गण्
def: v.t. To count, reckon, number, calculate, compute; to consider, deem, repute, esteem
subs: To count one's steps; — to move very slowly or with great caution
or ginne-lāʼiq, adj. To be counted, calculable, numerable.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

ety: Prk. गणत्तिआ; S. गण+आप्तिकां (i.e. आपि caus. aug.+ति+का
def: s.f. Counting, computing, calculating, reckoning; — calculation; enumeration; number; addition; the rule of addition (in Arith.); muster; muster-roll; — the first, or the last, day (of a month); — (fig.) reckoning, account, consideration, esteem (syn. shumār); care; value (for)
subs: adj. That can (or could) be counted; a small number, few (e.g. gintī-ke log the)
(-kī), To make a false roll or return (of)
(-kī), To take an account (of); to have (things) counted; to number; to muster
